message.body:
Aviation is in the spotlight - are you ready to take flight? 
 
Friday's Aspen Times put the airport on the front page.  
 
I stepped off the desk before the open and headed to Marble, where fate dropped two backcountry pilots in my path - one a director with the Recreational Aviation Foundation.  
 
The Aerial Economy keeps finding me. 
 
This week's note covers what I'm holding and watching:  
 
$ONDS pressing toward all-time highs 
 
$TSLA testing its multi-year breakout 
 
$DRNZ leading the drone trade 
 
​$APLD setting up for fresh highs 
 
I'm fully vested and patient. The frontier is taking flight. 
 
Read more here — https://www.godspeed.today/2026-05-31/deep-backcountry cc @howardlindzon

I couldn't help but notice the Aspen Airport grace the headline, front page of Friday's Aspen Times. Aviation is in the spotlight. Are you ready to take flight?

trends.summary: Investors are rotating into DRNZ as a concentrated thematic play on the burgeoning drone and autonomous defense sector. The community is actively discussing the fund's heavy exposure to key industry players and its potential to capture long-term growth driven by rising institutional defense contracts and advancements in unmanned aerial technology.

message.body:
$TSLA AI derived:Tesla has begun shipping a specific set of vehicles with high-end 8.3MP Samsung sensors, which include HDR (High Dynamic Range) and NIR (Near-Infrared) capabilities.
Cybercab likely being produced with an AI4.5 suite, including the full 8.3MP Samsung array(specifically tuned to handle edge cases—heavy rain, thick fog, and sun glare)-to move into that 99.9999% reliability tier in bad weather to obtain L4.

The 8.3MP Samsung lenses have a distinct deep red/purple "oil slick" coating.

$TSLA Cisco in 2000 at its peak, Cisco traded at a forward Price-to-Earnings (P/E) multiple of roughly 150x to 200x. Investors willingly paid this premium because they believed Cisco would monopolize the entire plumbing of the global internet.Tesla today because its auto earnings have slowed down while the stock price rises on hype, Tesla’s trailing P/E ratio has bloated to an astronomical 398x. Investors today are willingly paying that massive premium because they believe Tesla will monopolize global robotics, autonomous driving, and AI infrastructure. Longterm investors are gonna get fked dont say nobody warned you.

body: $TSLA Who remembers Cisco Systems  The hype was massive, the news was completely bullish and everyone argued that traditional valuation metrics were dead because Cisco was building the future global internet infrastructure. Cisco did end up building the future of the internet but the stock still crashed nearly 90% because the hype had driven the stock price completely out of line with REALITY . It took over 20 years for investors who bought the peak hype to break even. 😈 I can smell the blood from miles away and I cant wait to cash in on it

body: $BTC.X $TSLA $HOOD $SOL.X
$SPY $DJT $NVDA $TSLA $BTC.X

$NVDA $TSLA 
 
The Physical AI era is emerging: a ~$40T robotics revolution 
 
AI is shifting from software into embodied hardware intelligence. NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang sees humanoid robots unlocking a ~$40 trillion labor automation market, driving strong capital positioning. 
 
Physical AI is essentially digital intelligence with a body. Autonomous driving and humanoid robots share the same core stack of perception, planning, and control, with scale potential beyond smartphones and EVs. 
 
The market is forming around two pillars: NVIDIA as the infrastructure layer powering training and compute via chips and the Isaac platform; and Tesla as one of the few players pushing humanoid robots toward real commercialization. Capital inflows continue to signal an early but accelerating industrial cycle.

data.text:
The Physical AI era is emerging: a ~$40T robotics revolution 
 
AI is shifting from software into embodied hardware intelligence. NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang sees humanoid robots unlocking a ~$40 trillion labor automation market, driving strong capital positioning. 
 
Physical AI is essentially digital intelligence with a body. Autonomous driving and humanoid robots share the same core stack of perception, planning, and control, with scale potential beyond smartphones and EVs. 
 
The market is forming around two pillars: NVIDIA as the infrastructure layer powering training and compute via chips and the Isaac platform; and Tesla as one of the few players pushing humanoid robots toward real commercialization. Capital inflows continue to signal an early but accelerating industrial cycle.

message.body:
$TSLA trading through a messy 3 week stretch but the bigger picture still leans bullish.  
 
Structure hasn’t broken, dips continue to get absorbed and momentum still points toward a push back into price discovery.  
 
Main level to watch is the weekly 20 MA near 403, as long as that holds, path of least resistance stays higher with a retest of the 499 all time high still in play.  
 
Below that would shift the entire tone fast.  
 
Right now it feels more like digestion than distribution and bulls still have control of trend.

body:
This is wild, but it shows how markets reward extreme conviction in the right narrative.  
 
Back in June 2010, a 3000 dollar investment in the $TSLA IPO would have turned into a position worth over a million dollars today.  
 
The same 3000 dollars allocated to $BTC.X 
 
 in that same period would be around 5.35 billion dollars in value today.  
 
This is the definition of asymmetric upside in early phase innovation assets.  
 
Most traders miss it because volatility feels unbearable in real time and the story looks irrational.  
 
The real edge is staying positioned through deep drawdowns when conviction is tested and uncertainty dominates sentiment.

body:
$NVDA $TSM $TSLA 

Here is the thing I don’t get. 

Elon Musk is going to hop into fab production and build terrawatts of compute out of nothing. He doesn’t have any of these relationships that Jensen has been building for decades, and they are already ramping production as fast as they can. 

Musk wants to build chips and the entire supply chain is going to abandon Jensen and ramp up terrawatts of compute for Musk instead? 

Something doesn’t add up about that.

body: $TSLA

$SPY $QQQ $IWM $GM $TSLA

The following outlines a perspective on the incremental adoption of certain technologies and their potential implications for individual autonomy:

When livestock are guided through a chute, the design often incorporates curves to obscure the path ahead. This design encourages continuous forward movement, as the animals perceive the immediate threat behind them to be greater than the unknown ahead, despite the ultimate destination.

Let us consider a parallel in how control can be incrementally established and how individuals may progressively cede certain rights:

The following technological advancements represent a progression that has been largely accepted:

1. Lane departure warning systems: Accepted.
2. Automatic emergency braking: Accepted.
3. Adaptive cruise control: Accepted.
4. Autopilot functionalities: Accepted.
5. Monitoring cameras: Currently being accepted.
6. The absence of a steering wheel in vehicles: Pending.

data.title: Why Nobody Wants To Buy a Cybertruck (Anymore)
data.description: Ford killed the F-150 Lightning because nobody wanted it. Tesla's Cybertruck is selling even worse-but Elon Musk refuses to let it die. In this documentary, we uncover the hidden truth behind the spectacular collapse of the Tesla Cybertruck, how a promised $39,900 revolution turned into a depreciating six-figure nightmare, and how Musk is quietly using his own private rocket company to buy up unsold inventory just to keep the numbers from cratering.

body:
$TSLA I’m curious why there haven’t been massive class-action lawsuits filed in the US against Elon Musk and Tesla for their misleading and false advertising of Tesla owners and the public. They’ve been selling FSD capabilities for the past decade, claiming it’s capable of Full Self-Driving, but it’s still not level 4 autonomous. Calling and selling a service as Full Self-Driving for a decade that’s not even level 4 autonomous is criminal, to say the least. 🤷🏼‍♂️🤯 

https://seekingalpha.com/news/4598628-tesla-facing-consumer-lawsuit-in-china-over-fsd-claims-report

body:
$TE $FSLR $TSLA From the NY Times

First Solar Inc., a photovoltaic solar and manufacturing company, hired Mr. Trump’s campaign pollster, Fabrizio, Lee & Associates, in February to gauge how Republican voters felt about the technology. The findings: Most of them like solar, especially if the panels are made in the United States and without Chinese materials.

Separately, a group of solar companies formed a coalition called American Energy First and commissioned a poll by Kellyanne Conway, Mr. Trump’s longtime adviser. That survey found that 75 percent of Trump voters supported expanding U.S. solar energy, garnering a headline in Breitbart, a right-wing news outlet.

The solar coalition is also amplifying conservatives who have written recently about the benefits of clean energy, such as Newt Gingrich, the Republican former House speaker and an informal Trump adviser. 

https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/26/climate/solar-energy-politics-campaigns.html?smid=nytcore-ios-share

data.text:
From the NY Times

First Solar Inc., a photovoltaic solar and manufacturing company, hired Mr. Trump’s campaign pollster, Fabrizio, Lee & Associates, in February to gauge how Republican voters felt about the technology. The findings: Most of them like solar, especially if the panels are made in the United States and without Chinese materials.

Separately, a group of solar companies formed a coalition called American Energy First and commissioned a poll by Kellyanne Conway, Mr. Trump’s longtime adviser. That survey found that 75 percent of Trump voters supported expanding U.S. solar energy, garnering a headline in Breitbart, a right-wing news outlet.

The solar coalition is also amplifying conservatives who have written recently about the benefits of clean energy, such as Newt Gingrich, the Republican former House speaker and an informal Trump adviser.

Business with no profit. A trillion dollars handed over. No taxes paid.

user.bio: ALL about the SWING trades and waking up to GREEN. DAY trades are fun too, but limited. Will give a stock one day with overnight to show some green and will sell the next day. Bottom feeder as well as love playing failed ER/offering plays after hours
